eval_set.note: held-out provenance set; single-judge content+format scoring via tools/eval_local_model.py; CONTENT is the protected channel

fp16_base.note: FP16 base is deterministic (greedy decode); measured at 23/32 content in run #5 on the identical model+eval

combined_channel.note: format-and-content; religion format is consistently ~1/6 across all runs (eval-format quirk, not content), which caps religion's combined score

promotion.reject_reason: oraclePromote=false because invariant 'solver_attestation' is HELD (z3-solver not installed in the pod) — a missing-tooling abstention, NOT a quality/capability regression

result_summary: 3-epoch QLoRA produced a +12.5pt CONTENT uplift (71.9% -> 84.4%) over the FP16 base on this held-out provenance set, converting the 1-epoch content-neutral null into a directional positive. religion (protected) held flat; philosophy/psychology/history rose. The W2 oracle REJECTED promotion solely because z3-solver was unavailable for the solver_attestation invariant; every content/contamination/provenance/no-regression gate passed.
claim: real-GPU LoRA eval-ladder datapoint (NOT a capability claim)
judge: single-judge content+format scoring (tools/eval_local_model.py); NOT the kappa>=0.40 / 2-judge-family / >=3-run / 95%-CI VALIDATED bar
honest_scope: One real-GPU QLoRA run (3 epochs, seed 0, 32-item held-out provenance eval, single judge). The +12.5pt content uplift is DIRECTIONAL / candidate-only: at n=32, single judge, one seed, it is not separable from noise to the VALIDATED bar, and the training traces were religion-repair while religion (protected) held flat and OTHER domains rose (transfer or noise — undetermined at this power). It does NOT move canClaimAGI. The honest contrast with the 1-epoch run is the point: more training is the lever that moves content here, but a VALIDATED claim needs the 2-judge-family / kappa>=0.40 / >=3-run / 95%-CI protocol.

provenance.source: transcribed from the train-runpod step-7 eval-ladder + promotion report printed in the run log
